featuretablefilter 0.1.0
  • Get started
  • Reference

    Convert from phyloseq object to feature table data.frame

    from_phyloseq.Rd

    Extracts the OTU table from a phyloseq object and converts it to the standard data.frame format used by this package (feature IDs in first column, samples as columns).

    from_phyloseq(phylo_obj, transpose = TRUE, include_taxa = FALSE)

    Arguments

    phylo_obj

    A phyloseq object containing at least an OTU table.

    transpose

    Logical. If TRUE (default), transposes so features are rows and samples are columns. phyloseq stores OTU tables with features as rows by default, but some operations may change this.

    include_taxa

    Logical. If TRUE and tax_table is present in phyloseq, appends taxonomy information as additional columns after the feature ID.

    Value

    A data.frame with feature IDs in the first column, followed by sample counts. If include_taxa=TRUE, taxonomy columns are appended.

    Examples

    # if (requireNamespace("phyloseq", quietly = TRUE)) {
#   ps <- phyloseq::phyloseq(
#     phyloseq::otu_table(matrix(rpois(100, 5), 10, 10), taxa_are_rows = TRUE),
#     phyloseq::tax_table(matrix(sample(c("A","B","C"), 100, replace=TRUE), 10, 10))
#   )
#   df <- from_phyloseq(ps)
# }

    Contents
